out_of_scope_findings: game/content/items/sports_baseball_card.json contains a single item that duplicates content already in sports_memorabilia_cards.json — should be consolidated
game/content/stores/sample_sports_store.json appears to be an early draft that predates sports_memorabilia.json — may need cleanup
game/content/customers/casual_browser.json is a generic fallback customer not tied to any store type — unclear if this is intentional design or placeholder

Legacy single-item JSON files (sports_baseball_card.json, games_retro_cartridge.json, video_rental_vhs.json, fakemon_booster.json, electronics_mp3_player.json) are superseded by the full content sets and should be removed
sample_sports_store.json appears to be an older version of sports_memorabilia.json with different schema (starting_budget vs starting_cash, missing fixtures) — should be consolidated

The sports store definition uses store_type 'sports' but item files use store_type 'sports' — consistent, but the DATA_MODEL.md example uses 'retro_games' format. Video rental items use 'rentals' which matches the store def
No customer type definitions exist yet for pocket_creatures or electronics stores — these should be created in the next planning cycle

game/content/customer/ (singular) contains casual_browser.json while game/content/customers/ (plural) contains all store-specific customer files — directory naming inconsistency that DataLoader will need to handle or one directory should be consolidated

Store definition daily_rent values don't match the daily_rent_per_size values in pricing_config.json (e.g., rentals store is 'medium' with daily_rent $60, but pricing_config says medium = $120). Need to decide which is authoritative — per-store override or size-based default.

game/content/customer/ (singular) contains casual_browser.json while all other customer files are in game/content/customers/ (plural) — this directory inconsistency should be resolved as part of issue-086
video_rental_vhs.json was missing from issue-086's cleanup list — now added
The frontier assessment's coverage gap counts are stale — they report 1-2 items per store when actual counts are 19-46

The casual_browser.json uses singular 'customer' directory path in some references but actually lives in 'customers/' — the issue-020 reference to 'game/content/customer/casual_browser.json' is a stale path

Medium-size stores (rentals, pocket_creatures, electronics) have daily_rent of $55-65 but pricing_config.json says daily_rent_per_size.medium is $120 — inconsistency between store definitions and economy config
Legacy sports_memorabilia.json in stores/ has the same broken starting_inventory as the pre-fix unified file — confirms it's a stale duplicate that should be removed

Constants.STARTING_CASH is 5000.0 but store_definitions.json has per-store starting_cash ranging from 500-800. Need to decide which is authoritative — probably the store definition, with Constants as a fallback default. Not covered by any issue.

EventBus is missing several signals referenced in SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md: reputation_changed, money_changed, order_placed, order_delivered. These will need to be added incrementally as the corresponding systems are implemented, but there's no single issue tracking EventBus completeness.

casual_browser.json has a non-standard schema (spending_range instead of budget_range, missing store_types). Issue-086 notes this but doesn't fully resolve the schema mismatch. If kept, it needs a schema migration.

ProductDefinition resource (game/resources/product_definition.gd) exists but is not in RESOURCE_CLASS_SPEC.md and not referenced by any issue — may be legacy scaffold from initial repo setup. Should be evaluated for removal or formal inclusion.

game/autoload/event_bus.gd is missing inventory-specific signals that issues 005-007 will need (item_stocked, item_removed_from_shelf, item_added_to_inventory). These are documented in issue-005 but not yet added.
Constants.gd defines STARTING_CASH = 5000 but pricing_config.json and store_definitions.json use 500. This discrepancy will surface during issue-001/010 implementation.

The pricing_config.json uses daily_rent_per_size (small:50, medium:120, large:250) but store_definitions.json has per-store daily_rent values that don't match these size tiers (e.g., video_rental is medium but rent is $60, not $120). Need to reconcile which is authoritative — store-specific overrides or size-based defaults.

The casual_browser.json disposition is still unresolved (referenced in issues 020 and 086). It's a single-Dictionary format file that doesn't match any store. Decision needed: universal fallback customer type or delete as legacy.
Issue-036 (Progression system design) is the last remaining wave-2 design doc. It depends on issues 031 and 032, both now complete. Ready to be written next cycle.

The market_value formula has an ambiguity: base_price in content JSON already reflects rarity-appropriate pricing (a rare card is priced higher than a common), so applying rarity_multiplier again would double-count. Issue-010 update clarifies that M1 uses base_price * condition_multiplier only, with rarity_multiplier reserved for content generation and supplier pricing.

Issue-011 specifies NavigationAgent3D but notes M1 simplification of teleporting between BrowseZones. Issue-022 (customer pathfinding) should explicitly note it's upgrading this teleport to real navigation.

The EventBus signal item_sold(item_id, price) uses item_id (definition ID) but the inventory system tracks instance_id. This should be updated to item_sold(instance_id, sale_price) or include both. Flagging for issue-001 or a separate EventBus cleanup.

The explore agent reported incorrect item counts (18, 27, 25, 29, 31) vs actual (19, 28, 30, 38, 28) — the git status shows modifications to sports_memorabilia_cards.json and pocket_creatures.json, suggesting recent edits that the agent may have read from a stale state or miscounted
EventBus may need a time_speed_changed signal — issue-009 doesn't explicitly declare it but the HUD (issue-013) needs it. Should be confirmed during issue-009 implementation.

EconomySystem needs a get_daily_summary() method that issue-010's spec doesn't explicitly define — the day summary screen (issue-014) depends on it. Should be added to issue-010 during implementation.

The coverage gap assessment in the frontier reports '2 content items' per store, but actual counts are 19-38 per store. The assessment tool may be reading legacy single-item files instead of the full content sets. This should be investigated in the frontier assessment tooling.

DEPENDENCY_MAP.md critical path note omits the parallel track through 009 (TimeSystem) which also feeds into 011 (customer state machine). The true M1 critical path has two convergence points: 001→005 and 009, both feeding into 011.
Issue-086 (legacy file removal) was created this run but is not in the focus issues list — it should be a quick cleanup task after issue-001 is implemented
casual_browser.json disposition remains undecided — it could be a universal fallback customer or should be removed. Tracked in issue-086 notes but no concrete decision made yet
pricing_config.json on disk may have different content than what's in the spec — the update_file action provides the canonical version with all sections needed by wave-1 and wave-2 issues
Issue-059 depends on issue-027 (settings menu) but that dependency wasn't listed in the original — added it since the main menu has a Settings button that opens the settings panel
GameManager stub is missing DAY_SUMMARY and LOADING states from the architecture spec — issue-087 now covers this
EventBus stub is missing many signals that individual system issues will add — not a gap, each issue owns its own signal additions
DataLoader's load_all_json_in() currently only handles Dictionary results, not Array — issue-001 covers the full rewrite
InventorySystem stub uses quantity-based tracking incompatible with the design — issue-005 covers the rewrite
Constants.STARTING_CASH (5000) differs from economy config starting_cash (500) — should be reconciled during issue-010 implementation

StoreController base class is not explicitly created in any wave-1 issue — it will need to be extracted from the sports store during wave-1 or early wave-3. Consider adding a note to issue-004 or creating a small extraction task.
The food court area from MALL_LAYOUT.md has no implementation issue — it's atmosphere-only in M3 but should be explicitly noted as out-of-scope for issue-049 to prevent scope creep.
VHS degradation over 50-100 rentals (from VIDEO_RENTAL.md deep dive) is not tracked by any issue — could be part of issue-075 (rental lifecycle) in wave-5.

game/content/events/ directory is empty — no event definition JSON exists yet. Issue-053 (random daily events) and issue-034 (event/trend system design) cover this, but actual event content JSON will need to be created as a future content task, likely wave-3.

The frontier assessment's coverage gap detector appears to be reading legacy single-item files (counting 2 items per store) rather than the actual multi-item content files. The detector logic may need updating but that's a tooling concern outside the planning universe.

Content counts in issue files don't match actual file counts (sports: 23 on disk vs 20 in issue-015, retro: 29 vs 30 in issue-051). Minor discrepancy, not worth a planning action — DataLoader will be the source of truth.
Issue-087 lists 10 dependencies which may create a late-integration bottleneck. Consider whether some systems can be tested in isolation with mocks before full GameWorld integration.

Constants.STARTING_CASH is 5000.0 but store_definitions.json sports store has starting_cash of 500.0 — the constant appears unused (EconomySystem reads from store def), so not a bug, but the constant is misleading and should be removed or aligned in a future cleanup
Constants.DEFAULT_INTERACTION_RANGE is 3.0 but issue-003 specifies 2m range — the constant should be updated to 2.0 when issue-003 is implemented

The dependency map file at docs/production/github-issues/DEPENDENCY_MAP.md lists issue-012 deps as '010, 011' but the issue file itself says '004, 010, 011' — the map file is inconsistent with the source issue. Issue-004 is transitively covered via 011→004 but the map should match the issue

The ARCHITECTURE.md autoload table lists only 4 autoloads but issue-060 adds TransitionManager as a 5th. When issue-060 is implemented, ARCHITECTURE.md needs updating — but this is an implementation-time concern, not a planning gap.
project.godot already has 'interact' action registered (KEY_E), which issue-088 also lists as needing registration. Issue-088's audit should be updated to note this is already done.

game/content/events/ directory contains only .gdkeep placeholder — no event definitions exist. Issue-034 (design events/trends) is wave-2 and completed as a design doc, but no content JSON for events has been created yet. No issue tracks creating event content JSON.
pocket_creatures.json has 38 items but issue-067 targets 30-40 — within range but issue spec may reference a different count that should be verified
consumer_electronics.json has 28 items but issue-068 targets 20-25 — slightly over target, issue spec may need count update

Store starting_inventory counts vary widely (sports: 9, pocket_creatures: 23) — the pocket_creatures store starts with over half its items which may not match the intended progression curve from ECONOMY_BALANCE.md

Coverage gap assessment in the frontier is stale — reports 1-2 items per store but actual counts are 19-38. The assessment tool may be counting legacy single-item scaffold files instead of the unified content files.

Issue-001 references DataLoader access as 'DataLoader.get_item(id)' (global singleton style) but the decided architecture (issue-087 Option A) uses 'GameManager.data_loader.get_item(id)'. Issue-001 should be updated to clarify the access pattern once implementation begins, but the issue spec correctly focuses on the DataLoader class itself rather than how it's mounted.

The DATA_MODEL.md in docs/architecture/ is now significantly outdated vs RESOURCE_CLASS_SPEC.md and the actual JSON files — it references different field names, missing fields, and a different directory structure. It may warrant a future reconciliation pass but is low priority since RESOURCE_CLASS_SPEC.md is the authoritative spec.

ECONOMY_BALANCE.md tuning levers table says rarity_multipliers 'Scales high-end item values' which is misleading — should say 'Used for content authoring guidelines and future wholesale pricing, NOT applied to runtime market_value'. Low priority since it's a table cell, not a formula.
docs/design/CUSTOMER_AI.md was 'PATCH_ONLY' (1 line) despite issue-033 being marked DESIGN COMPLETE — suggests a previous file write was interrupted or the content was accidentally overwritten
Issue-022 had no mention of register queue behavior, but CORE_LOOP.md describes customers queuing — added queue design to the issue spec

The impulse_buy_chance field exists in all 21 customer type definitions but no issue previously specified how impulse buying works — documented in CUSTOMER_AI.md and it naturally falls into issue-021's scope
EventBus declares store_opened and store_closed signals that no wave-1 or wave-2 issue references — they may be needed for wave-3 (issue-046 store unlock) but are currently dead code
pricing_config.json has markup_ranges.default = 1.35 referenced by issue-010's get_suggested_price, but markup_ranges is not documented in DATA_MODEL.md's economy config schema
Issue 009 says input handling for speed keys could be delegated to GameManager — this decision isn't resolved. Recommendation: TimeSystem handles it directly since it owns time_speed state
sports_store_customers.json is a valid Array despite being flagged as single-dict by automated tooling — the frontier assessment coverage gap counts may also be stale
No issue covers creating a minimal test scene for validating player controller (issue-002) in isolation — implementers will need to create an ad-hoc test floor

The frontier scanner's 'Coverage Gaps' metric reports stores have only 1-2 items each — this appears to be using the legacy single-item scaffold files rather than the full content sets. The scanner should be updated to look at the correct content files (sports_memorabilia_cards.json instead of sports_baseball_card.json, etc.).

The EventBus currently uses Dictionary for customer_entered/customer_left params, but issue-011 and the signal registry specify typed String params. The signature change needs to happen in issue-088 pre-flight to avoid breaking existing code that might reference the old signatures.

Issue-087 (GameWorld integration) has 10 dependencies — it's effectively the wave-1 capstone. If any single issue slips, 087 is blocked. Consider whether 087 could be partially implemented with stub systems to enable integration testing earlier.
Wave-2 issues 024, 027, 028, 029, 030, 038, 039 were not checked for specification quality — they are not on the immediate frontier but should be verified before wave-2 implementation begins
Wave-3 implementation issues (045-057) may need more detailed specs when they become frontier items

issue-013 HUD displays reputation tier as formatted text (e.g., 'Local Favorite') but issue-018 stores tier as snake_case ID ('local_favorite') — need get_tier_display_name() which is now added to issue-018
issue-012 mentions WaitPosition Marker3Ds for customer queue but this is M1-simplified to one-at-a-time — the queue positions are scene infrastructure for future use, not a coordination gap
issue-008 price step calculation (_calculate_step) is unspecified but is internal to the pricing UI — not a cross-issue contract, can be resolved during implementation
